items.title: Brisbane's Lang Park to undergo largest upgrade in 23 years
items.description: The LNP government has announced plans for the largest upgrade to Lang Park in 23 years, featuring greater capacity, wraparound screens, and new entertainment zones ahead of next week's budget.

items.title: Italy's privacy watchdog fines Emirates over handling of passenger health data
FRANCE 24 Exclusive: what Strait of Hormuz locals think of the Iran-US deal
items.description: Since the US-Israel strikes on Iran began on February 28th the Strait of Hormuz has effectively been blocked - a key strategy by Iran that upended global oil supplies and pressured its opponents. But what has it meant for the seafarers stranded there? Or those who call its islands home? With plans for the key shipping channel to be fully reopened after Friday June 19, FRANCE 24's correspondent Reza Sayah headed out onto the water to talk with people in the region.

items.title: Censure motion against Tasmanian premier passes parliament
items.description: A censure motion against Jeremy Rockliff has passed, making him the first premier to be censured by the Tasmanian parliament.

items.title: Ramaphosa defends South Africa amid rising anti-immigrant tensions
items.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa has urged citizens not to blame migrants for the country's challenges, warning against attempts to exploit public frustrations amid a recent wave of anti-immigrant unrest and violence.

items.title: DRC: Ebola recoveries bring hope to fight against outbreak
items.description: Seven people said to have recovered from Ebola were released from a treatment centre in DR Congo on Tuesday, according to local health officials from the humanitarian organization Alliance for International Medical Action (ALIMA)

items.title: Aboriginal interpreters call for police engagement after inquest finding
items.description: More than a decade after Gene Gibson was wrongfully convicted for the murder of Broome tradesman Josh Warneke, Aboriginal interpreters say they are still overlooked by police.

items.title: PM Modi, German Chancellor Merz hold bilateral talks on trade and defence
items.description: We discussed possibilities for how we can further strengthen bilateral cooperation through joint efforts in the areas of trade, investments, circular economy, defence, IT, and other fields, said Prime Minister Narendra Modi

items.title: A nation determined to endure and overcome
items.description: In the 1970s, the U.S. actually encouraged Iran to develop nuclear power arguing that the country would eventually run out of oil

items.title: Moving from war to deal in a deeply divided region
items.description: A fragile Iran deal masks deeper regional rivalries and uncertainties

items.title: Norway's crown princess undergoes successful lung transplant, palace says
items.description: Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffered from pulmonary fibrosis, will now spend several weeks recovering in hospital.

items.title: ​​Messi World Cup sticker mania spurs black market in Argentina
items.description: As Lionel Messi competes in what could be his final World Cup, stickers featuring the fan favourite are in high demand, with adults and children caught in the craze. The popularity of Panini’s stickers have helped fuel shortages and driven up prices. Leer más

items.title: In US, EU mutual interest for Europe to use best AI models, von der Leyen says
Replay: Trump meets Modi, addresses memorandum of understanding in Iran and FRANCE 24's analysis
items.description: Rewatch as US President Donald Trump meets Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and addresses memorandum of understanding with Iran.

items.title: UK investigating warning shots fired by Russian ship at yacht
items.description: Russia appears to have fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel on June 16. British authorities say they are investigating the incident but so far treating it as isolated. The Grigorovich had been missioned with escorting tankers carrying Russian oil through the channel in the past. There were no reports of injuries or damage to the yacht.

items.title: U.S. not 'pulling away' from allies by cutting NATO commitments, says Rutte
items.description: U.S. and German media reported that the cuts include a third of the 150 U.S. F-16 and F-15 jets designated for NATO, plus refuelling and reconnaissance aircraft, bombers and drones

items.title: EU, G7 pledge support amid DRC Ebola outbreak
items.description: G7 leaders called for a "strong and coordinated response" to the latest Ebola outbreak. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen said that "health security is shared security." The recorded death toll in DRC is approaching 200.

items.title: Why Israel could still derail the Iran-US deal
items.description: Trump called it a historic deal. But with nuclear talks and Israel all unresolved, what did either side actually win?

items.title: Ángel Di María backs Argentina from afar: 'With you until the end of the world'
items.description: For the first time in 16 years, Ángel Di María will not lace up his boots at a World Cup, having appeared in four editions of the tournament. Leer más

items.title: Northern Lebanon airport revival brings economic hope for the region
items.description: Rene Mouawad Airport to serve Istanbul, Dubai, and Mersin as part of plans to revitalise northern Lebanon's economy.

items.title: Taiwan accuses Kenya of deporting conference delegates on China’s behalf
items.description: First African nation to host the global oceans conference defends deportations, saying it 'recognises only one China'.

items.title: 'End of an Era': Facing an increasingly adversarial US, Europe embraces proactive defence strategy
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome François Heisbourg, Chair of the International Institute for Strategic Studies. He argues that the apparent Iran-related "peace deal" announced by Donald Trump remains opaque, incomplete, and potentially unstable, with crucial details unresolved.

items.title: Israeli air strikes on Lebanon continue despite US-Iran deal
items.description: There has been a reduction in violence since the US-Iran agreement was announced, but attacks have not stopped.

items.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors in eastern Pacific Ocean
items.description: Southern Command said it "immediately notified U.S. Coast Guard to activate the Search and Rescue system for the survivors"

Israel takes control of Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque: What this means
items.description: Palestinians controlled planning and construction in the entire West Bank city under the 1997 Hebron Agreement.

items.title: Leader of South Africa's second biggest party wants his predecessor sacked as minister
items.description: John Steenhuisen is one of South Africa's best-known politicians after leading the DA for seven years.

items.title: Legal aid worker caught in bed with fugitive teen killer pleads guilty
items.description: A 37-year-old legal aid worker who was arrested while sharing a hotel bed with a teenage killer she helped evade authorities after he breached his parole has pleaded guilty to attempting to pervert the course of justice.

items.title: Replay: Macron holds press conference as G7 summit comes to an end
items.description: Rewatch French President Emmanuel Macron's press conference as the G7 summit comes to a close.

items.title: Despite agreement, G7 looks to decrease Strait of Hormuz reliance
items.description: A main topic of discussion among European leaders at the G7 summit has to do with decreasing the global reliance on the Strait of Hormuz. Iran’s success in shuttering the critical waterway has sparked fears that Tehran could use it as an ongoing bargaining chip if a ceasefire and peace deal falls apart. European leaders have pledged military assets to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Kinshasa priest engages DR Congo youth with rap music
items.description: Clad in his black cassock and energised by the frenetic beat, Jean-Pierre Mongambi, Kinshasa's "rapping priest", roamed underprivileged districts using rap to bring the gospel to the city's youth.

items.title: Australian shock jock wins A$12m payout after radio station tore up contract
items.description: Kyle Sandilands was sacked after an on-air spat with his co-host, who accused him of bullying.

items.title: Thousands flock to Art Basel in Switzerland as market outlook improves
items.description: Art Basel 2026 in Basel draws thousands as high-end works by Picasso, Warhol and Hockney fuel a recovering global art market after years of decline.

items.title: Magic Messi bags hat-trick, breaks World Cup record as Argentina rout Algeria 3-0
items.description: Lionel Messi opened his record-breaking sixth World Cup with a hat-trick to become the tournament's joint all-time top scorer on Tuesday as holders Argentina launched their bid for back-to-back titles with a dazzling 3-0 rout of Algeria. Leer más

items.title: 'Unprecedented' AI buildout drove Singapore's sharpest non-oil exports surge in over two decades: Analysts
items.description: Electronics exports nearly doubled in May, with analysts pointing to a concentrated surge in hyperscaler capital spending as the primary driver.

items.title: Sudan’s young women return to international soccer as war and taboos linger
items.description: Their red jerseys stood out against the green pitch. Most were teenage girls. Some had fled war. Others had never played in an organized soccer league or set foot in a major stadium before.

items.title: The bikers battling extreme heat and armed conflict to smuggle Iranian fuel to Pakistan
items.description: It's so hot the fuel can catch fire - the bikers tell the BBC they must live with the risk of injury and death.

items.title: Trump celebrates 80th birthday with Iran deal and UFC cage fight at White House
items.description: Donald Trump and UFC chief Dana White walked together from the Oval Office to the Blue Room Balcony to survey the Octagon and stood for the National Anthem as fighter jets thundered overhead. The crowd chanted “USA! USA!”

items.title: Temporary shelter becomes home for migrants fleeing unrest in South Africa
items.description: Displaced Malawians are being moved from a temporary shelter in Durban following a recent wave of anti-immigrant unrest in South Africa, with some migrants choosing to return home amid growing fears for their safety.

items.title: Japan raids ice cream giants over price-fixing allegations
items.description: The investigation on alleged cartel pricing of ice cream comes as Japan faces record summer temperatures.

items.description: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

Electronics firm Jabil raises annual profit forecast on strong data center demand
West Bank: Israeli settlers torch mosque
items.description: Israeli settlers set fire to a mosque in a West Bank village on Wednesday, local officials and witnesses said. Videos on social media showed the mosque in the town of Jiljilya ablaze while walls were marked with Hebrew graffiti reading “revenge”.

items.title: Trump calls Modi a ‘killer’ and an ‘angel’
items.description: President Donald Trump praised Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi as a “killer” and an “angel" at the G7 in France.

items.title: Trump doesn't rule out strikes on Iran despite agreement, looks to support Ukraine
items.description: The G7 summit has focused on the future of AI, and the memorandum of understanding between the US and Iran. As it comes to a close, its French host is hailing it as the most successful summit in years. Among its successes is bringing Trump behind Ukraine, as he changes his posture in favour of Kyiv and Russian sanctions. To do so, France is dealing an exchange between support to the US deal with Iran against US support for Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us what to know on that final day from Evian.

items.title: Girl, 2, dies in four-vehicle crash in West Melbourne
items.description: A two-year-old girl has died after a four-vehicle crash on a major arterial route in inner-city Melbourne this afternoon.

items.title: Ex-teacher's aide jailed over Snapchat messages sent to teens
items.description: A former Tasmanian teacher's aide, who used social media to send sexually explicit content to teenagers, has been sentenced to 15 months' prison.

items.title: Several Israeli strikes hit south Lebanon
items.description: Lebanon's Health Ministry raised the death toll in Israeli attacks since the war broke out to 3826, as rescuers pull more bodies from the rubble

items.description: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

items.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
items.description: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

items.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro called the conviction "baseless and senseless".

items.title: Fatal shooting of Russian anti-Putin artist likely politically motivated, Polish PM says
items.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said Wednesday that the fatal shooting of exiled Russian artist Semyon Skrepetsky was likely a political assassination. The Kremlin critic, known for provocative caricatures of President Vladimir Putin and other political figures, was shot dead by an unidentified gunman in eastern Poland on Monday.

items.title: There's blood in Blue water after NSW collapse in Origin II
items.description: New South Wales travelled to the MCG with the Origin series within their grasp but after the disaster that ensued, success has never felt further away.

items.title: 'AI agents' driving major shift in freelance hiring, Malt CEO says
items.description: Demand for skills linked to so-called AI agents has surged across Europe, according to new data from freelance marketplace Malt. At VivaTech 2026, CEO Vincent Huguet tells FRANCE 24 how companies are racing to hire AI talent, why demand is spreading beyond the tech sector and what this means for the future of work.

items.title: South Africa play Czechia, seek first World Cup win after Mexico defeat
items.description: After losing their opening group matches, both teams need a victory to keep knockout hopes alive.

items.title: China, Nepal FMs hold 'comprehensive' talks, discuss bilateral ties
items.description: At the meeting, Mr. Wang Yi said China has always placed its relations with Nepal in an important position in its neighbourhood diplomacy and pursues a friendly policy toward all Nepalese people

items.description: The 28-year-old is accused of killing an American health insurance executive. If successful, his defence could see him convicted of manslaughter rather than murder.

items.description: Authorities say a recent two-week compliance blitz results in them seizing $11 million of illegal product like cigarettes and vapes, but how much is it disrupting the lucrative industry?

items.description: Ellyse Perry has starred with bat and ball as Australia gave their net run-rate a massive boost with a landslide win over Bangladesh at Headingley in the T20 World Cup.

items.description: For Somaliland, the embassy opening represents a diplomatic victory and could strengthen its campaign for broader international recognition. For Israel, it marks the deepening of ties with a strategic partner in the Horn of Africa.

items.description: The AFL hands down a fine to the Suns after the club's fifth incident this season of a player making careless contact with an umpire.

items.description: Britain’s Defence Ministry described it ⁠as ‘an isolated incident’ and not linked to the interception of ‌a Russian shadow fleet vessel by British commandos

items.title: Messi becomes joint World Cup all-time scorer with hat trick against Algeria
items.description: Argentina beat Algeria 3-0 on June 16. Lionel Messi's hat trick of goals came 20 years to the day after his World Cup debut. Messi is now the first man to play in six World Cups and the tournament's joint all-time scorer, alongside Germany's Miroslav Klose, with 16 goals. The player was praised by supporters on both sides.

items.title: Tehran War Diary: Shooting war at home
items.description: An internationally acclaimed photojournalist in Iran turns his lens on his own city, as Tehran comes under bombardment.

items.title: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
items.description: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

items.title: Fear of Ebola keeps pregnant women away from hospitals in DR Congo
items.description: Health professionals warn that avoiding medical care could have serious consequences for mothers and their babies.

items.title: 1 killed in plane crash on Texas highway
items.description: Police say six people were onboard and one was killed. The plane crashed in Laredo shortly after 10 p.m., said Jose Baeza, an investigator with the Laredo Police Department

items.title: US firms sue Teo Siong Seng, shipping container makers over alleged price-fixing scheme
items.description: The suits from US companies were filed against Singamas, China International Marine Containers, CXIC Group Containers and a group of executives, including Mr Teo.

items.title: NT's new climate resilience plan lists gas project as top priority
items.description: The Northern Territory government's newly released climate resilience plan has listed the acceleration of gas extraction at the Beetaloo Basin as a top priority. Environment groups have fiercely criticised the plan, saying it will "make climate change worse".

items.title: South Korea to shrink security buffer zone to North Korea
items.description: South Korea will ease restrictions on roughly 270 square kilometers of land near the border with North Korea. Farmers in the area had long complained of rules that complicated working conditions.

items.title: DR Congo’s Ebola outbreak tops 800 cases as death toll reaches 192
items.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo’s Ebola outbreak continues to worsen, with confirmed infections climbing to 808 and the death toll reaching 192. Health authorities are battling the spread across three eastern provinces while isolation and response efforts remain underway.

items.title: DR Congo Ebola peak 'in front of us', outbreak could last a year, says Red Cross
items.description: ‘The peak is, I think, not beyond us, but in front of us; we are afraid that this could last one year’ before ending the outbreak, says Bruno Michon, the IFRC's operations manager for the Ebola outbreak

items.title: Netherlands hosts Japan’s emperor Naruhito to boost 400-year ties
items.description: The Netherlands welcomed Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako to Amsterdam on 17 June during an official visit aimed at celebrating the long-standing relationship between the two countries.

items.title: Allbirds rebrands as Smartbird in AI pivot, hires former AWS executive as CEO; shares soar
Watch: California wildfires rage near passing vehicles
items.description: The fires in Riverside County cover over 2,000 acres, say authorities.

items.title: How Herat defied the Taliban regime, chanting ‘women, work and freedom’
items.description: The protests in the Afghan city in the first week of June were triggered by the enforcement of a dress code, which requires women to wear burqa in public; many women, including those wearing traditional Afghan wear of manto, were arrested, but released later as entire communities protested

items.title: France examines bill to rein in ultra-fast-fashion's expansion
items.description: Here in Paris, Chinese fast-fashion brand Shein is leaving the iconic BHV department store in the chic Marais neighbourhood. The departure follows months of controversy over its inclusion at the landmark shop - with critics accusing Shein's low-cost, high turnover business model of destroying French retail, and high-end brands like Dior and Guerlain abandoning BHV. A French parliamentary committee begins on June 17 an examination of a proposed law to rein in the expansion of ultra-fast fashion.

items.description: Argentina take on Algeria in Kansas City, while France face Senegal in New Jersey. Leer más

items.title: Messi ties World Cup goals record as Argentina tops Algeria
items.description: Remarkably, the hat trick came exactly 20 years after Messi made his World Cup debut. It was also his fifth consecutive World Cup match with a goal, further strengthening his claim as football’s greatest player.

items.title: Nigerian man jailed for storing human faeces outside his home
items.description: Neighbours said the stench from the property had become so overwhelming that it was impossible to relax at home.

items.title: Floyd Mayweather faces theft charges for using bad cheque to buy a watch
items.description: Boxing legend allegedly wrote the cheque on New Year's Eve 2024 and could face up to 20 years in jail if found guilty.

items.title: Israeli settlers torch mosque in West Bank village of Jiljilya
items.description: Residents of Jiljilya, north of Ramallah, awoke on 17 June to find their mosque set alight overnight, with burnt tyres, Hebrew graffiti reading "revenge" and extensive damage left behind in an area of the occupied West Bank where Israelis are prohibited from entering.

items.title: UK orders Google to improve transparency for search services
items.description: British businesses complain Google's current ranking practices are neither fair nor transparent.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Africa's new era takes shape

Tunisia repatriates nearly 100 sub-Saharan migrants in 'voluntary return'
items.description: Tunisian authorities repatriated nearly a hundred migrants from sub-Saharan Africa on Tuesday under a so-called "voluntary return" initiative that has involved almost 5,000 people over the past year, Tunisia's national guard said.

items.title: Big Tech-backed coalition for carbon removal increases funding by $915 million, adds Anthropic
G7 summit hailed a success by Macron thanks to developments on Ukraine and Iran
items.description: Ukraine has won key pledges of further support from world leaders in defending itself from Russia's full-scale invasion, now in its fifth year. Leaders attending the summit promised to strengthen Ukraine’s air defenses and ensure its energy supply, as well as step up international economic pressure on Moscow. This comes as US President Donald Trump, bolstered by the memorandum of understanding with Iran, shifted away from Moscow. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us what to know.

items.title: Africa urgently needs more fish farms, UN tells ocean conference
items.description: Africa needs to urgently expand its fish-farming sector to meet its food needs, the head of the UN's fisheries division said Tuesday, even as its latest report found record production levels globally.

items.title: Sydney-to-London unveiled as first route for Qantas's Project Sunrise
items.description: The flights, set to begin take off from next year, will mark the first time flights from the east coast will complete the Kangaroo Route without a stop over.

items.description: Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for China's Taiwan Affairs Office, said Taiwan was engaging in "intelligence theft, infiltration, and sabotage activities, escalating cross-strait confrontation and undermining cross-strait relations

items.title: Russian artist and outspoken Putin critic shot dead days after protest
items.description: Russian artist was shot dead in Poland just three days after a performance protest in Berlin near the Russian embassy.

items.description: Mr. Khanal's visit to Beijing followed closely on his trip to India during which he said his government would accord “highest priority” to relations with New Delhi

items.description: Reopening of vital oil route would mark a major step toward ending months of deadly conflict and economic turmoil triggered by US-Israeli strikes on Iran in late February. Leer más

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Abdullah Ibrahim, South Africa’s World-Renowned Jazz Maestro, Dies at 91
items.description: Legendary South African pianist and composer Abdullah Ibrahim has died at 91, leaving behind a remarkable musical legacy shaped by exile, resistance and cultural identity. Discovered by Duke Ellington, his compositions became synonymous with the anti-apartheid movement and global jazz.

items.title: No hantavirus found in Mendoza Province after tests, says Health Ministry
items.description: Hantavirus probe in western Mendoza Province did not find any virus-carrying rodents, announces Argentina's Health Ministry. Leer más

items.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
items.description: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

items.title: NATO chief focuses on alliance unity after US alters pledges
items.description: NATO's secretary general went to great lengths to cast a positive light on the alliance ahead of a ministers' meeting. The closest he came to addressing any tension was saying members have "a lot to talk about."

items.description: Under its new president, Bolivia is re-establishing severed ties with the US to combat organised crime.

items.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
items.description: Electric cars are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever. China is driving the transition, but Europe and other countries are catching up fast.

items.title: Tunisia fire coach Lamouchi, appoint Renard after heavy opening World Cup loss
items.description: Tunisia have sacked head coach Sabri Lamouchi on Monday after the team’s 5-1 defeat against Sweden in their opening World Cup game.

items.title: European Parliament approves US trade deal to lower tariffs
items.description: The European Parliament has approved cutting tariffs on many US imports to comply with a deal signed last year. US President Trump had given the bloc a July 4 deadline to meet its side of the deal.

items.title: India: Telegram to challenge government order blocking app
items.description: Telegram has reportedly challenged the Indian government's order to temporarily block the popular messaging app ahead of the NEET medical entrance exam.

items.title: 'Greatest feeling ever': Cape Verdeans tell BBC of joy at holding Spain to draw
items.description: The streets of the capital, Praia, shook with wild celebrations as the small island nation held Spain to a shock 0-0 draw.

items.title: U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors
items.description: The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing one man and leaving two survivors. This brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes to at least 208.

items.title: The long-term implications of the U.S.-Iran deal
items.description: Irrespective of largely unaltered borders and an endgame that has barely commenced, the past three years of hostilities have triggered seismic geopolitical changes in West Asia and beyond

This time round, it appears Paris is focused on showcasing its ties with Washington, rather than fixating on their recent barbs.

items.title: Elon Musk's AI tool Grok was used in strikes against Iran, says U.S. government
items.description: At the end of February, the U.S. government terminated its contracts with Anthropic after it refused to allow its tools to be used for fully automated strikes or the mass surveillance of Americans

items.title: U.S. President Donald Trump to wrap G7 summit facing skepticism at home, jitters overseas over his plan to end Iran war
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is also expected to meet with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi at a choppy moment in the U.S.-India relationship

items.title: Pro-Palestine solidarity on display at the 2026 World Cup
items.description: Fans from Algeria to Bosnia are making sure the Palestinian cause gets attention during the World Cup.

items.title: Former CAR president faces crimes against humanity trial
items.description: A landmark trial is set to begin in the Central African Republic, where former president Francois Bozize will be tried in absentia on charges of crimes against humanity.

items.title: Argentina fans deploy 'asado' to challenge Kansas City's BBQ crown
items.description: Argentina begin their World Cup defence in Kansas City, the self-styled global barbecue capital, but their fans insist they're already king of the grill. Leer más

items.title: Russia strikes leave historic Kyiv cathedral in flames
items.description: The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was set on fire as Russia struck multiple cities in Ukraine overnight. Meanwhile, Moscow reported three deaths from a Ukrainian drone attack.

items.title: Iconic Paris canal opens to swimmers as residents seek ways to cope with heatwave
items.description: As France is bracing for another heatwave, Paris is allowing swimming in one of its canals so residents could cope with the heat. It will be the second spell of hot temperatures this year, after an unusually scorching week in May. Later in the summer, swimmers will also be able to cool off at designated points along the Seine River. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell tells us more from the canal.

items.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
items.description: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

items.title: Israeli ambassador to U.S. says Israel is 'not going to withdraw from South Lebanon'
items.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Israel's ambassador to the U.S. Michael Leiter about the peace deal the Trump administration says it's made with Iran.

items.title: Mining threatens scarce water resources in the Andes
items.description: San Juan Province is facing a persistent water shortage due to climate change, while mining advances across the Andes and its thousands of glaciers. Leer más

items.title: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
items.description: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.

items.title: World Cup: Cape Verde hero and Iran suffer US travel woes
items.description: After starring in Cape Verde's draw with Spain, goalkeeper Vozinha shared his sadness that an expensive travel bond stopped his mother from attending. On the same day, Iran's coach said his team were being "oppressed."

items.title: Why the Williams sisters and other stars are playing longer
items.description: More than three decades after they burst onto the tennis scene, Venus and Serena Williams are still playing at the top level. Why are we seeing elite athletes carrying on into their 40s and beyond?

items.title: Meloni quits smoking, Trump gets jersey: Hot mics at G7 capture world leaders' chats between weighty topics
items.description: Mr. Trump added some levity after Macron appeared to have left his watch behind when he departed the group's working lunch

items.title: Argentina calls up Marcos Senesi for World Cup after Balerdi injury
items.description: Marcos Senesi has been added to the squad as a replacement for Leonardo Balerdi, who has been ruled out of the tournament with a muscle injury. Leer más

items.title: Ships remain on hold in Strait of Hormuz despite announced US-Iran deal
items.description: Hundreds of ships remained at a standstill in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, as the United States and Iran reached an initial agreement that would extend their shaky ceasefire and lead to the reopening of the strait.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Not all skinny: The French do get fat
items.description: With the French healthcare system now reimbursing the anti-obesity drugs Wegovy and Mounjaro, we explain what the strict conditions are to benefit from them. We discuss France's rates of obesity and the areas where they're highest. Finally, we discuss the common stereotype that French people don't get fat. While not true, it does contain a sliver of truth when comparing France to other EU nations.

items.title: Donald Trump and his Ukrainian bête noire
items.description: Hard as it is for Trump to stomach, Zelenskyy has greatly distinguished himself as a war leader. As for Trump, unless he is very lucky, he could find his name permanently attached to a deal with the Iranians that will bring to mind Neville Chamberlain. Leer más

items.title: Ukraine: Russian drone attack on Zaporizhzhia home kills one, injures seven
items.description: At least one person was killed and seven others injured after a Russian drone struck a residential building in the southeastern Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia on 16 June, sparking a major fire and causing extensive damage, Ukrainian officials said.

items.title: France seal hard-earned win against Senegal, Messi scores hat-trick for Argentina
items.description: Tactical changes by Les Bleus manager Didier Deschamps at half-time saw France seal a 3-1 win in their opening game against Senegal. Kylian Mbappé scored a brace, becoming the country's top goalscorer, with 58 goals to his name. Meanwhile, Lionel Messi helped Argentina shine with a hat-trick against Algeria – enough to put pressure on Cristiano Ronaldo, whose Portuguese side will face DR Congo this Wednesday?

items.title: Tireless human rights activist Taty Almeida, founding member of Madres de Plaza de Mayo, dies at 95
items.description: Teacher, iconic human rights activist, president of Madres de Plaza de Mayo Línea Fundadora and campaigner for the disappeared and downtrodden. Taty Almeida, who has died at the age of 95, would not be silenced in her tireless search for memory, truth and justice and the knowledge over what happened to her missing son Alejandro. Leer más

items.title: Germany start 2026 World Cup with win over Curacao
items.description: For the first time since they won the tournament in 2014, Germany won their opening game at the World Cup.

items.title: Argentina launch bid for back-to-back title in Messi's sixth World Cup
items.description: Algeria will be Argentina's first hurdle in Group J, while Austria and Jordan await in Dallas on June 22 and June 27. Leer más

items.title: South Africa's DA drops ex-leader Steenhuisen from cabinet
items.description: South Africa's Democratic Alliance, the second-largest party in the fragile coalition government, on Wednesday demoted former leader John Steenhuisen from the cabinet months before local elections

items.title: West Asia highlights: Iran says talks on final U.S. deal to begin this week
items.description: U.S.-Iran peace deal to be signed at Switzerland's Burgenstock resort on Friday (June 19, 2026)

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: June 5 to 12
items.description: A selection of stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Electricity like milk? The plan to simplify complex power bills
items.description: Millions of Australians have been often unwittingly moved to complex and variable power tariffs. An energy watchdog says bills must be simplified.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz reopens: But can ships’ safety be assured?
items.description: Shipping operators and insurers prefer to wait and watch from a distance for now.

items.title: G7 Leaders tackle Ukraine war and Middle East crisis in high-stakes summit
items.description: World leaders gathering at the G7 summit in France face high-stakes talks on two major global crises: the war in Ukraine and instability in the Middle East.

items.title: 'Moment of grief': Pakistani police apologise for 9yo Australian girl's death
items.description: Pakistani officials apologise for the fatal shooting of a nine-year-old Australian girl, and outline the violation of protocols that led to her death as they pursue murder charges against the officer involved.
